message:
✨ Shared repo filter bar for /ci-cd pipeline cards (#8424)

* ✨ Shared repo filter bar for /ci-cd pipeline cards

Adds a PipelineFilterProvider + PipelineFilterBar that wraps the /ci-cd
dashboard. All 4 pipeline cards (Pulse, Matrix, Flow, Failures) now
read from a shared repo selection instead of each managing their own.

### How it works

- PipelineFilterProvider (React Context) holds the shared repoFilter
  state and the server-driven repo list.
- PipelineFilterBar renders a pill-toggle row at the top of /ci-cd
  (via DashboardPage's headerExtra slot). "All" + one pill per repo.
- Each card calls usePipelineFilter(). If the context exists (on the
  /ci-cd dashboard), the card uses the shared state. If null (card
  dragged to a different dashboard), it falls back to its own per-card
  <select> dropdown.

### Behavior

- Single repo configured (PIPELINE_REPOS=myorg/myrepo): one pill, no
  "All" toggle, dropdown has one entry.
- Multiple repos: "All" + individual pills. Click a pill to filter;
  click again to deselect (back to "All"). All 4 cards update in sync.
- Per-card dropdown still works as an override when a card is on a
  different dashboard where the provider is absent.

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* 🔧 Update ai-non-localized baseline to 188 (matches main after recent merges)

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* 🔧 Update hex color ratchet baseline to 270 (matches main after recent merges)

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

---------

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

+ Repos +
+ {showAll && ( + + )} + {repos.map((repo) => { + const short = repo.split('/')[1] ?? repo + const isSelected = repoFilter === repo + return ( + + ) + })} +
+
+ ) +}

@@ -0,0 +1,53 @@
+/**
+ * PipelineFilterContext — dashboard-level shared filter state for the
+ * GitHub Pipelines cards. When the /ci-cd dashboard wraps its cards in
+ * <PipelineFilterProvider>, every pipeline card reads the shared repo
+ * selection instead of managing its own.
+ *
+ * Cards that are dragged to OTHER dashboards (where this provider is
+ * absent) fall back to their own per-card state — the hook returns
+ * `null` and the card renders its own <select>.
+ */
+import { createContext, useContext, useState, useCallback, type ReactNode } from 'react'
+import { getPipelineRepos } from '../../../hooks/useGitHubPipelines'
+
+interface PipelineFilterState {
+  /** null = "All repos", string = single repo filter */
+  repoFilter: string | null
+  setRepoFilter: (repo: string | null) => void
+  /** The repo list (read from the server on first fetch, then cached) */
+  repos: string[]
+}
+
+const PipelineFilterCtx = createContext<PipelineFilterState | null>(null)
+
+/**
+ * Wrap the /ci-cd dashboard in this provider to give all pipeline cards
+ * a shared repo filter. Cards call `usePipelineFilter()` to read it.
+ */
+export function PipelineFilterProvider({ children }: { children: ReactNode }) {
+  const [repoFilter, setRepoFilter] = useState<string | null>(null)
+
+  const value: PipelineFilterState = {
+    repoFilter,
+    setRepoFilter: useCallback((repo: string | null) => setRepoFilter(repo), []),
+    repos: getPipelineRepos(),
+  }
+
+  return (
+    <PipelineFilterCtx.Provider value={value}>
+      {children}
+    </PipelineFilterCtx.Provider>
+  )
+}
+
+/**
+ * Returns the shared filter state if inside a PipelineFilterProvider,
+ * or null if the card is on a different dashboard. Cards should check:
+ *
+ *   const shared = usePipelineFilter()
+ *   const repoFilter = shared?.repoFilter ?? localRepoFilter
+ */
+export function usePipelineFilter(): PipelineFilterState | null {
+  return useContext(PipelineFilterCtx)
+}

@@ -3,3 +3,5 @@ export { WorkflowMatrix } from './WorkflowMatrix'
 export { PipelineFlow } from './PipelineFlow'
 export { RecentFailures } from './RecentFailures'
 export { LogsModal } from './LogsModal'
+export { PipelineFilterProvider } from './PipelineFilterContext'
+export { PipelineFilterBar } from './PipelineFilterBar'
